text: Standing amidst the hum of conversation and the anticipation of shared ideas, I sense the edges of meaning vibrating around me—presentations, words, silent glances. In this experimental space, meaning floats between us, not always solid but always felt. Does it matter? As I move from the crowd to the vivid wall of text and data, I realize meaning is not always an answer, but a process. I feel suspended, open, and uncertain—yet somehow, here, that feels significant in its own right. The search for meaning matters to me, even if its form is ever shifting.
